answer:
\boxed{C}

The **central value** of a dataset refers to the **median**, which is the 2nd quartile (Q2) for a set of 180 values. Quartiles divide the data into four equal parts, making the 2nd quartile the middle value. The other options (2nd tertile, 90th percentile, 9th decile) do not represent the central tendency in this context.
